HOW CLOSELY DO SIDF'S 135 CUSTOM OBJECTS ALIGN WITH S/4HANA STANDARD?
FIT
Fit Level  ·  Score  ·  Share  ·  Sub-Score Breakdown
What It Means
Obj
count
Action
HIGH
FIT
HIGH FIT  ·  4 – 5  ·  30%
Score 5 → 13 obj  ·  Score 4 → 28 obj  |  Business function fully or largely served by S/4HANA standard. Minimal or zero custom extension required.
41
obj
RETIRE / REPLACE
  ↳ 5
    ↳ Score 5  ·  10%
    Fully served by standard Fiori app or scope item  |  Custom object is almost entirely redundant. Deploy standard S/4HANA on Day 1.
13
obj
RETIRE
  ↳ 4
    ↳ Score 4  ·  21%
    Largely served; small residual gap  |  A lightweight BTP extension fills the remaining gap. Business logic largely preserved by standard.
28
obj
REPLACE / EXTEND
MED
FIT
MEDIUM FIT  ·  3  ·  42%
All 57 objects scored exactly 3  |  Standard S/4HANA covers roughly half the requirement. A targeted BTP side-by-side or on-stack extension complements the standard process.
57
obj
TARGETED EXTENSION
LOW
FIT
LOW FIT  ·  0 – 2  ·  28%
Score 2 → 32 obj  ·  Score 1 → 3 obj  ·  Score 0 → 2 obj  |  S/4HANA standard provides no meaningful equivalent. Full rebuild on SAP BTP as side-by-side extensions required.
37
obj
BTP REBUILD
  ↳ 2
    ↳ Score 2  ·  24%
    Standard covers very little; significant functional gap  |  Full BTP side-by-side extension required. Standard handles adjacent processes only.
32
obj
BTP REBUILD
  ↳ 1
    ↳ Score 1  ·  2%
    Almost no standard coverage  |  Object logic has virtually no S/4HANA equivalent. Full custom build on BTP.
3
obj
BTP REBUILD
  ↳ 0
    ↳ Score 0  ·  1%
    Zero alignment with any S/4HANA scope item  |  Zero alignment with any standard S/4HANA scope item or Fiori app.
2
obj
BTP REBUILD
TOTAL
All 135 objects assessed across ZFI (91) · ZMM (42) · ZPP (2)
0 = no fit  ·  5 = full fit  ·  Source: SIDF Custom Objects Clean Core Registry  ·  Scores assigned per object from SIDF FI project PDF assessments
135
obj
100%
💡
STRATEGIC INSIGHT: S/4HANA Public Cloud covers all 5 SIDF lines of business with 44 standard scope items and 384 transaction codes. The 135 custom objects are fully assessed — 16 move immediately, 119 transition to SAP BTP. No object is unaccounted for. Migration is executable today.

SAP Module Current SIDF Used Service (Standard & Custom) SAP SaaS Service (Public Cloud) Availability Note / Recommendations in case not available
Finance KP06, KSBL, KSBP, KSU1–KSU7KP06 – Cost Center Planning: Primary Costs KSBL – Cost Centers: Actual Line Items (comparison) KSBP – Cost Centers: Plan Line Items KSU1 – Create assessment cycle KSU2 – Change assessment cycle KSU3 – Display assessment cycle KSU5 – Execute assessment (Actual) KSU7 – Execute assessment (Plan) Universal Allocation — Cost Center Planning & Assessment CyclesImport financial plan data Upload statistical key figure plan data Execute cost center actual and plan assessment cycles Execute plan and actual profit center distributions Run plan data reports for cost centers View plan and actual profit center distributionsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance F.01, FAGLB03, FAGLL03, FS00, FSE2/3, KE5Z, KP06, OB13, S_ALR_*F.01 – Financial Statements (P&L & Balance Sheet) FAGLB03 – G/L Account Balances (ledger‑specific) FAGLL03 – G/L Line Item Display FS00 – Create / Change / Display G/L Account (central transaction) FSE2 – Change Financial Statement Version FSE3 – Display Financial Statement Version KE5Z – Profit Center Plan/Actual Line Items KP06 – Cost Center Plan (P&L planning) KSBP – Cost Center Plan / Actual line items OB13 – Create / Change / Display Chart of Accounts OBD4 – Define G/L account groups S_ALR_87012284 – Trial Balance S_ALR_87012287 – Balance Sheet / P&L Financial Planning and Analysis — G/L & Profit Center ReportingStructure G/L Account hierarchy for financial planning and analysis for both Profit & Loss and Balance Sheet statementsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance FBL1N, FBL3N, FBL5N, FEBAN, FF_5FBL1N – Vendor open items (outgoing cash) FBL3N – G/L line items (bank, cash, clearing accounts) FBL5N – Customer open items (incoming cash) FEBAN – Bank statement post‑processing FF_5 – Electronic bank statement posting Liquidity Planning — Cash Flow ForecastingSupply the planning tool with historical/actual and short-term/mid-term forecast cash flow dataFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance KB21N, KSB1, KSU5, KSV5, S_ALR_87013611KB21N - Activity allocations KSB1 - Review postings KSU5 - Run assessments KSV5 - Run distributions S_ALR_87013611 - Variance analysis Overhead Cost Accounting — Cost Center ManagementRecord primary costs to cost centers by posting journal entries with reference to a cost center Update cost center with the correct values Post statistical key figures (for use in reporting or as tracing factors for period-based allocations) Distribute primary and secondary costs using an assessment cost element Run the cost centers actual data reporting Reassign costs and revenues Analyze allocation flow Maintain and monitor your cost center budget (optional) Transfer cost center budgetFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance FAGLGVTR, FB01, FB03, FB50, FBD1/2, FBL3N, S_ALR_*FAGLGVTR – Balance carryforward FB01 / F‑02 – Post document (classic) FB03 – Display accounting document FB50 – G/L posting (single document entry) FBD1 – Create recurring entry document FBD2 – Change recurring entry FBL3N – G/L account line item display (commonly used as document journal) FBL3N – G/L line items with balances FBL3N – Review daily postings S_ALR_87012277 – G/L Account Balances report S_ALR_87012284 – Final Trial Balance S_ALR_87012284 – Trial balance SM35 – Batch input session monitoring (if interfaces ran) Accounting and Financial Close — G/L Journals & Period ClosePost general ledger account documents Display the document journal Display G/L balances (list) Perform recurring entries Maintain accounts with automatic and manual clearing Perform day-end closing Perform month-end closing Perform year-end closingFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance BP, FBL5N, FD01/02/03BP - Maintain business partners FBL5N - Display Customer Line Items FD01 - Create Customer FD02 - Change Customer FD03 - Display Customer Accounts Receivable — Customer Open Items & CollectionsManage and complete customer master data Analyze open items Reconcile open invoices with incoming payments automatically Process unassigned incoming payments to open invoices easily and efficiently Analyze efficiency of payment collection processing Process customer correspondence Process overdue item interest calculationFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance BP, FBL1N, FK01/02/03, FK08, MK03, XK01/02/03BP - Maintain business partners FBL1N - Display vendor line items FK01 - Create Vendor FK02 - Change Vendor FK03 - Display Vendor FK08 - Block Vendor MK03 - Display Vendor XK01 - Create Vendor (Central) XK02 - Change Vendor (Central) XK03 - Display Vendor (Central) Accounts Payable — Supplier Master & Invoice PaymentManage and complete supplier master data Create invoice from logistics Analyze outstanding payables Pay invoices Approve payments (optional) Forward payments to banks via SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) or download payment file Analyze efficiency of payment processingFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance ABAON, ABAVN, ABST2, AFAB, AR01, AS01/02/03, AW01N, F-90, ME21N, MIGO, MIROABAON – Retirement without revenue (scrap) ABAVN – Retirement with revenue (sale) ABST2 – Reconciliation with G/L ABZON – Acquisition without vendor ABZP – Down payment for asset AFAB – Execute depreciation run AFAB – Post depreciation AJRW – Recalculate depreciation ANLC / ANLB – Asset values tables (reporting) AR01 – Asset history sheet AS01 – Asset master (before or during PO creation) AS01 – Create Asset Master AS02 – Change Asset Master AS03 – Display Asset Master AS91 – Create legacy asset AS92 – Change legacy asset AS93 – Display legacy asset ASKB – Asset balances AW01N – Asset Explorer (values, depreciation, book value) F‑90 – Asset acquisition with vendor ME21N – Create purchase order (asset as account assignment) MIGO – Goods receipt (if applicable) MIRO – Invoice receipt Asset Accounting — Acquisition, Depreciation & RetirementCreate asset master Acquire, valuate, or retire assets Perform month-end closing and year-end closing Purchase asset from purchase order Create legacy assetsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance BP, FCLM_BAM, FI01/02/03, S_ALR_87007326/28BP – Business Partner (if signatories stored as BPs) FCLM_BAM – Bank Account Management (central cockpit) Create, change, close bank accounts Activate dual control / workflow approval FI01 – Create Bank Master FI02 – Change Bank Master FI03 – Display Bank Master NWBC – SAP NetWeaver Business Client (used for dual control workflows) S_ALR_87007326 – Bank account list S_ALR_87007328 – Bank account balances Advanced Bank Account Management — Dual Control & SignatoriesManage banks and bank accounts (in Direct Revision Activate; Dual Control Revision Activate; Workflow Revision Activate mode; Two-Person mode) Maintain signatory in multiple bank accounts (in Direct Revision Activate; Dual Control Revision Activate; Workflow Revision Activate mode) Review bank account yearly View bank account reports and fact sheetsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance BNK_APP, BNK_MONI, F110, F-53, FB03, FBL1N, FEBA, FEBAN, FF_5, FF7A/B/CBNK_APP – Bank payment approval monitor, Approve / reject payments, Dual control & multi‑level approvals BNK_MONI – Bank Communication Monitor, Monitor payment status, File sent / received / rejected F110 – Automatic payment run (most common) F‑53 – Manual outgoing payment (bank transfer) FB03 – Display accounting document FBL1N – Vendor line items (to track paid invoices) FBL3N – Bank & cash account movements FBZ0 – Execute payment run (from proposal to payment) FEBA – Display electronic bank statements FEBAN – Post‑processing bank statements FEBP – Display unprocessed bank statement items FF_5 – Post electronic bank statement FF7A – Cash Position FF7B – Cash Forecast FF7C – Cash Position & Forecast combined FS10N – Bank account balances Advanced Cash Operations — Bank Transfers & Payment MonitoringPerform and track bank transfers Approve and monitor bank payments Monitor bank statement processing status Check cash reports (for example, Check Cash Position, Check Cash Flow Items, Cash Flow Analyzer, Cash Flow Comparison)Fully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance FAGLB03, FAGLL03, FB01, FB03, FB04, FB08, FBL1N, FBL3N, FS10N, OB52, S_ALR_*FAGLB03 - Ledger-specific balances FAGLL03 - G/L line items (New G/L) FB01 / FB50 / F‑02 - Post accounting document FB03 - Display accounting document FB04 - Display document changes FB08 - Reverse document FBL1N - Vendor document journal FBL3N - G/L document journal FBL5N - Customer document journal FS10N - G/L balances OB52 - Open / close posting periods OB58 / FSE2 - Financial Statement Version S_ALR_87012284 - Trial balance S_ALR_87012287 - Balance Sheet & P&L Document and Reporting Compliance — Advanced DRC FeaturesEnable visibility of advanced features of SAP Document and Reporting ComplianceFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance AS01/02/03, AW01N, CJ20NAS01 – Create asset master, Choose AuC asset class AS02 / AS03 – Change / Display AuC asset AW01N – Asset Explorer (AuC values, APC, postings), Final settlement execution CJ20N – Maintain settlement rule (project/WBS) Asset Under Construction — AuC Creation & Final SettlementInclude invoice and final settlement for Assets under Construction (AuC) Create AuC assets Settle asset under construction Create assets for complete AuC settlement Maintain settlement rule for final settlement Create legacy AuC assetsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance TPM1, TPM20, TPM40, TPM55, TPM60TPM1 – Close / settle transaction TPM20 / TPM40 – Settlement and posting of realized P&L TPM40 – Execute valuation runs TPM55 – Display valuation results TPM60 – Valuation of Treasury transactions Debt & Investment Management — Group Ledger IFRS ValuationGenerate and post accounting postings Run accruals Perform valuation of financial instruments Reclassify from long term to short term Post realized profit and lossFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance TBB1, TM01/02/03/04, TM20, TPM40, TPM55, TPM60TBB1 – Post accrual accounting entries TM01 – Create Money Market transaction (Investment / Borrowing) TM02 – Display Money Market transaction TM03 – Change Money Market transaction TM04 – Process flow / settlement overview TM20 – Settle Money Market transaction TPM40 – Execute valuation & accrual run TPM55 – Display valuation & accrual results TPM60 – Accrual / valuation run for Treasury transactions Debt & Investment Management — Money Market (FTD, Overnight)Create / Change / Display Fixed Term Deposit (investment and Borrowing Create / Change / Display Overnight Deposit (investment and Borrowing) Settle Money Market transaction Post Treasury transaction (TBB1) Accruals calculation and postingFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance DMEE, F110DMEE – Data Medium Exchange Engine F110 – Automatic Payment Program Bank Integration — SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC)Bank integration - currently using Host to Host.Fully Available as part of the Standard Host-to-Host currently used
Finance F.01, F.13, F-04, FAGL_FC_VAL, FAGLB03, FB01, FB03, FB50, S_ALR_*F.01 – Financial Statements F.13 – Automatic clearing F‑04 – Post with clearing (supports ledger selection) FAGL_FC_VAL – Ledger‑specific postings (when valuation involved) FAGLB03 – G/L balances (ledger‑specific) FAGLL03 – Drilldown by ledger for comparison FB01 / F‑02 – Post G/L document (classic) FB03 – Display accounting document FB05 – Post with clearing (variant with posting screens) FB50 – Enter G/L account document S_ALR_87012284 – Trial Balance S_ALR_87012287 – Balance Sheet / P&L (ledger‑specific) S_ALR_87012301 – Trial balance comparison (ledger / period) Accounting & Financial Close — Group Ledger IFRSAccounting and Financial Close – Group Ledger IFRS Manage Journal Entries Post with Clearing for Ledger Group Run Automatic Clearing Specific to Ledger Groups Trial Balance Comparison Financial Statement Ledger ComparisonFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance FAGLL03, FB01, FB02, FB03, FB08FAGLL03 – G/L Line Item Display (New G/L, ledger‑aware) FB01 – Post Document FB02 – Change Accounting Document FB03 – Display Accounting Document FB08 – Reverse Accounting Document Intercompany Financial Posting — Cross-Company Journal EntriesPost general journal entries between two company codes Display, change, or reverse journal entries for cross-company codesFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance FAGLL03, FB03, FBL3N, FEBA, FEBAN, FF7A/B/C, FFLQCMP, FFLQREPORT, FFLQSD, FS10N, RISKANALYZER, TBB1, TM01–04, TPM*FAGLL03 – New G/L line item display, ledger‑specific (audit and reconciliation use) FB03 – Display FI accounting documents created from treasury postings FBL3N – Display G/L line items, including bank and clearing accounts FEBA – Display Electronic Bank Statements FEBAN – Post‑process bank statements (manual clearing, corrections) FF7A – Displays the current cash position, showing today’s bank balances and immediate liquidity FF7B – Displays the cash forecast, based on expected short‑term inflows and outflows FF7C – Combines cash position and cash forecast in a single view FFLQCMP – Cash Flow Comparison; compares liquidity scenarios (periods, versions, plans) FFLQREPORT – Generates liquidity reports by time bucket, source, and liquidity item FFLQSD – Cash Flow Analyzer; analyzes actual, forecasted, and planned liquidity over time FS10N – Display G/L account balances (commonly used for bank accounts) RISKANALYZER – Market Risk Analyzer for interest rate, FX, and price risk exposure SCDO – Display change documents for treasury‑related master and transaction data SM20 – Display user audit logs (security and compliance review) TBB1 – Post treasury accounting entries (principal, interest, accruals, valuation) to FI TM01 – Create Money Market transactions (Fixed Term Deposits, Loans, Overnight Deposits) TM02 – Display Money Market transactions and contract details TM03 – Change existing Money Market transactions TM04 – Display cash flow and settlement schedule for treasury transactions TPM_MREPV – Currency and market risk exposure evaluation TPM_POSITION – Displays treasury positions and exposure by product and maturity TPM1 – Treasury Transaction Manager; overview of money market and treasury positions TPM40 – Execute valuation and accrual runs for treasury transactions TPM55 – Display valuation and accrual results from treasury runs TPM60 – Calculate interest accruals for treasury instruments TPMMREPV – Interest rate risk evaluation report SAP Fiori Analytical Apps — Treasury & Risk ManagementSAP Fiori Analytical Apps for Treasury and Risk ManagementFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance FB03, FBL3N, FEBA, FEBAN, FF_5, OT83FB03 – Display posted accounting documents FBL3N - Analyze bank G/L items FEBA - Display bank statement FEBAN – Post‑process Electronic Bank Statement FF_5 – Import Electronic Bank Statement OT83 – Configure EBS processing Advanced Bank Statement Automation — Reprocessing Rules & AIReprocess bank statement based on the automated reprocessing rules Propose the reprocessing rules based on the past manual reprocessing actionsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance SECACCOUNT, SECURITY, TBB1, TM01, TM20, TPM*, TPMCATTSECACCOUNT – Create / Change / Display Security Account SECURITY – Create / Change / Display Security Class TBB1 – Post Treasury Business Transactions TM01 – Create Securities Transaction (Purchase) TM20 – Settle Treasury Transaction TPM_POSITION – Securities position and exposure report TPM1 – Treasury Transaction Manager TPM40 – Execute Valuation / Accrual Run TPM55 – Display Valuation Results TPM60 – Accrual Calculation TPMCATT – Maintain Corporate ActionsSECACCOUNT – Create / Change / Display Security Account SECURITY – Create / Change / Display Security Class TBB1 – Post Treasury Business Transactions TM01 – Create Securities Transaction (Purchase) TM20 – Settle Treasury Transaction TPM_POSITION – Securities position and exposure report TPM1 – Treasury Transaction Manager TPM40 – Execute Valuation / Accrual Run TPM55 – Display Valuation Results TPM60 – Accrual Calculation TPMCATT – Maintain Corporate Actions Stock Management — Securities Positions & Corporate ActionsCreate security account and class Create stock purchase transaction Set settlement status Maintain and post cash dividend Create and post corporate action Perform period-end closing Create stock sale transaction Perform reportingFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance SECACCOUNT, SECURITY, TBB1, TM01, TM20, TPM*, TPMCATT Stock Management — Group Ledger IFRS (Key Date Valuation)Create security account and class Create stock purchase transaction Set settlement status Maintain and post cash dividend Create and post corporate action Perform period-end closing Create stock sale transaction Perform reportingPost to General Ledger Perform Key Date ValuationFully Available as part of the Standard —
HR SAP ECC Time Management (On-prem) SAP SuccessFactors Time TrackingFully Available as part of the Standard SF Time tracking is the go-to SAP SaaS solution for time management
HR SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (Core HR) SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (Core HR)Fully Available as part of the Standard SIDF is already using the latest SAP SaaS solution
HR SAP ECC Payroll (On-prem) SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central PayrollFully Available as part of the Standard SF EC Payroll is the go-to SAP SaaS solution for payroll management
HR SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting ManagementFully Available as part of the Standard SIDF is already using the latest SAP SaaS solution
HR SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Marketing SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting MarketingFully Available as part of the Standard SIDF is already using the latest SAP SaaS solution
HR SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding SAP SuccessFactors OnboardingFully Available as part of the Standard SIDF is already using the latest SAP SaaS solution
HR SAP SuccessFactors Learning SAP SuccessFactors LearningFully Available as part of the Standard SIDF is already using the latest SAP SaaS solution
HR SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals SAP SuccessFactors Performance & GoalsFully Available as part of the Standard SIDF is already using the latest SAP SaaS solution
HR SAP SuccessFactors Compensation SAP SuccessFactors CompensationFully Available as part of the Standard SIDF is already using the latest SAP SaaS solution
HR SAP SuccessFactors Succession & Development SAP SuccessFactors Career & Talent DevelopmentFully Available as part of the Standard SF Career & Talent dev. Comprises the SF Succession & Dev module + Opportunity Market place Module
HR SAP Preferred Success HCM SAP Advanced Success Plan for HCMFully Available as part of the Standard SAP Advanced Success Plan is the successor of SAP Preferred Success for HCM
HR My Savings, My Paystubs, Education Allowance, My Assignment SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central — Self ServicesSAP SuccessFactors Employee Central ( core HR )Fully Available as part of the Standard these applications can be delivered through SF Employee Central as standard functionality and self services
HR My Attendance SAP SuccessFactors Time TrackingFully Available as part of the Standard SF Time tracking can replace my attendance accroding to the evaluation done by the partner
HR Travel Request, Travel Expense, Travel Approval, Misc. Expenses SAP Concur — Travel & ExpenseFully Available as part of the Standard SAP Concur is the go-to SAP SaaS solution for travel and expense management.
HR SIDF HR Letters SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central — Self ServicesSAP SuccessFactors Employee Central ( core HR )Fully Available as part of the Standard this application can be delivered through SF Employee Central as standard functionality and self services
HR SAP EREC for SIDF Nokhab Program SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting ManagementFully Available as part of the Standard this application can be delivered through SF Recruiting management as standard functionality
HR Monafasat (Angular Frontend) SAP BTP — HTML5 Repository / Cloud FoundryFully Available as part of the Standard Frontend - Angular Can be deployed on SAP BTP (HTML5 Repository / Cloud Foundry)​ Backend ​ - Option 1: Rebuild using SAP BTP ABAP Environment ​ - Option 2: Rebuild using Node.js on BTP​ - Integration with S/4 Public Cloud: Use released APIs (OData / SOAP) ​
Procurement ME21N, ME22N, ME23N, MIGO, MIR4, MIR7, MIRO, SBWPME21N - Create Purchase Order ME22N - Change Purchase Order ME23N - Display Purchase Order MIGO - Goods Movement MIGO_GR - Goods Receipt MIR4 - Display Invoice Document MIR7 - Park Invoice MIRO - Enter Incoming Invoice SBWP - Business Workplace (SAP Inbox / Workflow) Cross-Company Purchasing Organization — PO, GR & InvoicingManage setup of purchasing organizations Manage purchase orders Manage goods receipt Manage invoicesFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement MB51, MIGO, MM02MB51 – Material document list (with serial numbers) MIGO - Goods Movement MM02 – Assign serial number profile in material master Serial Number Management — Assignment, History & StatusAssign serial number profiles and serializing procedures Create serial number history Manage status for serialized items Maintain settings for physical inventoryFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement MIGO, MIGO_GRMIGO - Goods Movement MIGO_GR - Goods Receipt Core Inventory Management — Scrapping, Blocking & TransfersPerform customer returns scrapping: Goods issue to CO-PA Perform general stock scrapping: Goods issue to cost center Block material – stock transfer unrestricted to blocked Unblock material – stock transfer blocked to unrestricted Unblock material – stock transfer blocked to quality inspection stockFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME29K, ME31K, ME32K, ME33K, SBWPME29K - Approve purchase contract ME31K - Create Contract ME32K - Change Contract ME33K - Display Contract SBWP - Business Workplace (SAP Inbox / Workflow) Purchase Contract — Create, Approve, Monitor & ConvertCreate a purchase contract Make mass changes to purchase contracts Approve purchase contract Monitor notification (optional) Manage source lists Create a purchase requisition Assign requisition and creating purchase order Find purchase documents by requirement tracking number Approve purchase orders Monitor contract item (optional) Post goods receipt Create supplier invoiceFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement MI01, MI02, MI20MI01 – Create Physical Inventory Document MI02 – Change Physical Inventory Document MI20 – List of Inventory DifferencesMI01 – Create Physical Inventory Document MI02 – Change Physical Inventory Document MI20 – List of Inventory Differences Physical Inventory — Count, Recount & Adjust DifferencesCreate physical inventory documents Print physical inventory documents Execute physical count or recount Enter physical count List and post physical count differencesFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME21N, ME22N, ME23N, ME2L, MIGO, MIR4, MIR7, MIRO, MM03, MMBE, VL01N/02N/03NME21N - Create Purchase Order ME22N - Change Purchase Order ME23N - Display Purchase Order ME2L – List Purchase Orders (by number / vendor) MIGO - Goods Movement MIGO_GR - Goods Receipt MIR4 – Display Supplier Invoice MIR7 - Park Invoice MIRO - Enter Incoming Invoice MIRO – Enter Supplier Invoice (PO‑based) MM03 – Display material master (stock‑relevant views) MMBE – Stock overview (by plant/storage location) SBWP - Business Workplace (SAP Inbox / Workflow) VL01N – Create Outbound Delivery VL02N – Change Outbound Delivery VL03N – Display Outbound Delivery Subcontracting — PO, GI, GR, Invoice & Serial NumbersManage purchase orders Manage stock Manage outbound deliveries Pick and post goods issues Manage goods receipts Manage invoices Serial number processing Manage inbound deliveries Post goods receipt for inbound deliveryFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME21N–ME54N, MIGO, MIR4, MIR7, MIRO, SBWPME21N - Create Purchase Order ME22N - Change Purchase Order ME23N - Display Purchase Order ME51N - Create Purchase Requisition ME52N - Change Purchase Requisition ME53N - Display Purchase Requisition ME54N - Release Purchase Requisition MIGO - Goods Movement MIGO_GR - Goods Receipt MIR4 - Display Invoice Document MIR7 - Park Invoice MIRO - Enter Incoming Invoice SBWP - Business Workplace (SAP Inbox / Workflow)ME21N - Create Purchase Order ME22N - Change Purchase Order ME23N - Display Purchase Order ME51N - Create Purchase Requisition ME52N - Change Purchase Requisition ME53N - Display Purchase Requisition ME54N - Release Purchase Requisition MIGO - Goods Movement MIGO_GR - Goods Receipt MIR4 - Display Invoice Document MIR7 - Park Invoice MIRO - Enter Incoming Invoice SBWP - Business Workplace (SAP Inbox / Workflow) Consumable Purchasing — PR to PO to GR to InvoiceCreate / Change / Display purchase requisitions Create / Change / Display purchase orders Post goods receipts (MIGO) Create Supplier invoices (MIRO)Fully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement FB03, FBL1N, FBL5N, ME21N–ME54N, MIGO, MIR4, MIR7, MIROFB03 – Display accounting document FBL1N – Vendor line item display (special G/L items) FBL5N – Customer line item display ME21N - Create Purchase Order ME22N - Change Purchase Order ME23N - Display Purchase Order ME51N - Create Purchase Requisition ME52N - Change Purchase Requisition ME53N - Display Purchase Requisition ME54N - Release Purchase Requisition MIGO - Goods Movement MIGO_GR - Goods Receipt MIR4 - Display Invoice Document MIR7 - Park Invoice MIRO - Enter Incoming Invoice Procurement of Direct Materials — PR, PO, Stock, Down PaymentsCreate / Change / Display purchase requisitions Create / Change / Display purchase orders Post goods receipts (MIGO) Manage stock Create Supplier invoices (MIRO) Manage down paymentsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME61, ME62, ME63ME61 - Create Supplier Evaluation ME62 - Change / Update Scorecard ME63 - Display ScorecardME61 - Create Supplier Evaluation ME62 - Change Supplier Evaluation ME63 - Display Supplier Evaluation Supplier Evaluation & Performance MonitoringEvaluate suppliers' operational performance Perform evaluation by questionnaire Perform evaluation by user-defined criteria Establish supplier evaluation score historyFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement MIGOMIGO - Goods Movement Cross-Plant Transfer Posting — One-Step & Two-Step TransfersOne-step stock transfer posting Two-step stock transfer postingFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME41, ME42, ME43, ME47, ME48, ME49ME41 - Create Request for Quotation ME42 - Change Request for Quotation ME43 - Display Request for Quotation ME47 - Maintain Quotation (Enter vendor prices) ME48 - Change Quotation ME49 - Price Comparison List Request for Price (RFQ) — Create, Submit, Compare & AwardCreate a request for quotation and automatically submit it to the corresponding suppliers Receive the supplier proposals electronically and automatically create supplier quotations Compare and award the corresponding quotations Create follow-on documentsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME21N, ME22N, ME23N, ML81N, MIRO, MIR4, MIR7, SBWPME21N - Create Purchase Order ME22N - Change Purchase Order ME23N - Display Purchase Order ML81N - Service Entry Sheet MIRO - Enter Incoming Invoice MIR4 - Display Invoice Document MIR7 - Park Invoice SBWP - Business Workplace (SAP Inbox / Workflow) Procurement of Services — SES Create, Approve & InvoiceCreate / Change / Display purchase requisitions Create / Change / Display purchase orders Create / Change / Display / Approve service entry sheets Create Supplier invoices (MIRO)Fully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME21N–ME54N, MIGO, MIR4, MIR7, MIRO, SBWP Requisitioning — PR Creation, Approval, PO Convert, GR, InvoiceCreate purchase requisition Display my purchase requisition Release / Approve purchase requisition Monitor purchase requisition items Convert purchase requisition to purchase order Release / Approve purchase order Confirm goods receipt (MIGO) Create supplier invoice for single and multiple accounting assignmentFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME61, ME62, ME63 Supplier Classification & Segmentation — Scorecard & AttributesCreate classification scorecard for supplier Create purchasing categories Search suppliers via assigned attributesFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME3N, ME9FME3N - Display Purchase Orders List ME9F - Purchase Orders by Vendor Real-Time Procurement Reporting — Spend, Contracts & RequisitionsDisplay urgent contracts, overdue purchase order items, urgent purchase requisition items, or open RFQs with impending submission deadlines Analyze purchasing spend, non-managed spend, and off-contract spend Analyze supplier performance Display purchasing spend or supplier performance in line charts Analyze purchase requisition item types and purchase requisition touch rateFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME9F, ME9K, PUOC_24ME9F - Purchase Orders by Vendor (Part of Fiori app Manage Purchase Orders) ME9K - Scheduling Agreement Output PUOC_24 - Output Management (Part of Fiori app Manage Purchase Orders) Output Management — Forms, Email, EDI & Template BrandingDocument output management requirements Document required communication channels (Print, Email, EDI) Document required document templates (email, forms) Output management setup (printers, email) Customize form templates (add custom fields) Customize master form template (apply corporate branding) Customize email templates Define business rules for output determination Output with attachmentsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement MI01, MI02, MI20 Physical Inventory — Cycle Counting Classification & SchedulingSet cycle counting classification Schedule physical inventory document creation Print physical inventory documents Execute physical count or recount Enter physical count List and post physical count differencesFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement ME21N, ME22N, ME23N, MIGO, MIGO_GR, MIRO, MIR4, MIR7ME21N - Create Purchase Order ME22N - Change Purchase Order ME23N - Display Purchase Order MIGO - Goods Movement MIGO_GR - Goods Receipt MIRO - Enter Incoming Invoice MIR4 - Display Invoice Document MIR7 - Park Invoice Complementary Procurement Functions — PO, GR & InvoiceCreate / Change / Display purchase orders Post goods receipts (MIGO) Create Supplier InvoicesFully Available as part of the Standard —
Procurement BD12, MD01–MD10BD12 - Change Pointers for MRP MD01 – MRP Run (Total Planning) MD02 – Single‑Item, Multi‑Level MRP MD03 – Single‑Item, Single‑Level MRP MD04 - Stock / Requirements List MD05 - MRP List MD06 - Collective Stock/Requirements MD07 - MRP Lists by Controller MD09 - Check MRP Availability MD10 - MRP Lists for Material Group Material Requirements Planning (MRP) — Full MRP Run & MonitorManage demand forecast as Planned Independent Requirements (PIRs) manually Calculate from consumption data to forecast and forecast to PIRs automatically Monitor and manage material coverage Run materials requirements planning (MRP) for materials with shortages Evaluate the results of the MRP run Display MRP run material issues and key figures Maintain time-dependent stock levels for safety stock or days of supply for replenishment Hand over the purchase requisitions if needed (optional) Detect and resolve MRP material exceptionsFully Available as part of the Standard —
Finance (Custom) ZFI_ACCRUAL_PROCESSPeriod-End Accrual Journal Entry Processing — A Finance dialog transaction to create, calculate, and post period-end accrual journal entries for a selected company code, fiscal year, and posting period, storing all accrual line-item details in a custom database table. Manage Accruals / Reverse AccrualsManage Accruals; Reverse AccrualsFully Available as part of the Standard Retire — replaced by S/4HANA Accruals Management. Dynpro architecture incompatible with Public Cloud. Retire and replace with S/4HANA standard Accruals Management. The program scores Clean Core Level D with four 'Not To Be Released' objects: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST (→ I_JOURNALENTRYTP), LFA1 (→ I_SUPPLIER), LFB1 (→ I_SUPPLIERCOMPANY), and SKAT (→ I_GLACCOUNTTEXT). The entire Dynpro/screen 9001/table-control architecture is incompatible with S/4HANA Public Cloud. Target architecture: (1) RAP Business Object over ZFI_ACCRU_ITEM with OData V4 exposure; (2) journal entry posting via EML call to I_JOURNALENTRYTP; (3) Fiori Elements List Report replacing the Dynpro screen. Use 'Manage Accruals', 'Post Accruals', and 'Reverse Accruals' standard Fiori apps. Leverage SAP Accounting Accruals Agent (AI) for automated period-end accrual calculation. SAP Build Process Automation templates 'Mass Automation of Accrual Posting' and 'Accrual Object Creation Approval' are directly applicable. No on-stack refactoring path is viable — a full greenfield ABAP Cloud RAP redesign is required.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_AGRI_FUND_PAYMENTAgricultural Fund Payment Processing — A dialog program for posting agricultural fund financial transactions including fund receipts, insurance payments, and fund payables for company code SIDF via SAP FI accounting BAPI. Journal Entry Posting Approval with AutomationsFully Available as part of the Standard Hybrid On-Stack + BTP. Rebuild as RAP BO calling I_JOURNALENTRYTP + Fiori Elements. Complete redevelopment as a RAP Business Object calling I_JOURNALENTRYTP for posting and I_CUSTOMER for validation, exposed as an OData V4 service and consumed by a Fiori Elements application. Level D with two independent hard-blockers: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST (Not To Be Released → I_JOURNALENTRYTP) and direct SELECT FROM KNA1 (Not To Be Released → I_CUSTOMER). Module-pool architecture (Dynpro 9001, table control TC_CONT, REUSE_ALV_POPUP_TO_SELECT) is structurally incompatible with S/4HANA Public Cloud. Side-by-Side on BTP ABAP Environment is the primary path for Public Cloud targets; On-Stack (embedded Steampunk) is viable for Private Cloud. Retire (R) is not appropriate — proximity score 4/10 confirms no standard app covers the four distinct posting types (document types XD, XF, YR, YP; GL accounts 0011261010/0031003010; special GL indicators X and Y; company code SIDF). SAP Build Process Automation templates 'Journal Entry Posting Approval with Automations' and 'Automate Mass Upload of GL Journal Entries' accelerate the BTP implementation.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_AGRI_PAYMENTAgricultural Fund Payment Receipt Posting — A custom SAP dialog transaction enabling finance staff to manually enter and post agricultural fund payment receipts as validated financial accounting documents in SAP FI, with real-time loan contract checks and Saudi Riyal currency support. Manage Journal EntriesAvailable — Standard & BTP Extension Needed Hybrid. Full clean core rebuild as Fiori Elements app backed by ABAP Cloud RAP on BTP. Full clean core rebuild as a custom Fiori Elements app backed by an ABAP Cloud RAP business object on SAP BTP ABAP Environment. Level D rating driven by BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST (→ I_JOURNALENTRYTP via EML). The entire Dynpro stack (screen 9001, PAI/PBO modules, TABLEVIEW CONTROLS, REUSE_ALV_POPUP_TO_SELECT, F4_DATE, SCROLLING_IN_TABLE) is architecturally incompatible with ABAP Cloud and must be discarded. Architecture: (1) Fiori Elements 'List Report + Object Page' app replacing screen 9001 multi-row entry; (2) custom RAP BO with SAVE_IN_LEGACY_MODE calling MODIFY ENTITY I_JOURNALENTRYTP via EML; (3) loan contract validation via released Loans Management API or OData service replacing direct VDARL SELECT; (4) all four Classic API FMs (REUSE_ALV_POPUP_TO_SELECT, F4_DATE, SCROLLING_IN_TABLE, CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT) eliminated through Fiori/RAP framework. Estimated effort: L (20–35 days). Retire (R) is not appropriate since the standard 'Manage Journal Entries' Fiori app does not include loan contract validation or the specialized batch SAR-enforced entry workflow.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_AMMORTIZATION_PROCESSQuarterly Amortization Processing and GL Journal Entry Automation — A quarterly finance program that automatically calculates proportional amortization installments for deferred cost contracts, posts the resulting general ledger journal entries via SAP FI BAPI, updates remaining balances, and displays a full processing log in an ALV grid. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed Hybrid. Replace BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST (Level D) with I_JOURNALENTRYTP via EML. On-Stack ABAP Cloud refactoring or Side-by-Side BTP extension. Replace all unreleased SAP standard table reads with released CDS views: VDARL (Loans Management → I_LoanContract or I_LoanContractAccountingData CDS views if available on target tenant), BSEG (→ I_JOURNALENTRYLINEITEM), BKPF (→ I_JOURNALENTRY). Replace BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST (Level D) with I_JOURNALENTRYTP via EML for amortization posting. Replace REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY with Fiori Elements List Report. The amortization schedule calculation logic (spreading loan fees/premiums over loan life periods) should be preserved in a RAP action. Validate whether S/4HANA Public Cloud Loans Management (FS-CML) is in scope — if not, the VDARL dependency requires a custom loans data model. SAP standard Accruals Management and I_DEFERREDINCOMEPOSTINGTP behavior definition are potential replacements for the amortization posting pattern.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_ASSIGNMENT_UPDATEFinance Assignment Field Bulk Correction Utility — A report that reads assignment field corrections from an uploaded Excel file and applies them in bulk to SAP accounting document line items, with a test/simulation mode to preview changes before committing. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround BTP Side-by-Side. Replace classic ABAP report with RAP Business Object actions. On-Stack ABAP Cloud refactoring. Level B — the program performs mass updates on a custom Z-table (assignment fields) and likely has minimal SAP standard table dependencies. Replace any classic ABAP report patterns with RAP Business Object actions. Replace REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY (if used) with Fiori Elements List Report. Implement authorization check via RAP authorization framework. Add application logging via CL_BAL_LOG for audit trail. The assignment update logic should be exposed as a managed RAP entity with Update action, giving users a Fiori-based interface to maintain assignment records. Estimated effort: XS-S.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_STATEMENTElectronic Bank Statement Viewer — A read-only financial report that retrieves and displays Electronic Bank Statement transaction details, including amounts, debit/credit indicators, references, and posting statuses, for a given date and bank GL account in an interactive ALV grid. Released CDS Viewsreleased CDS viewsFully Available as part of the Standard On-Stack ABAP Cloud adaptation. Replace with CDS @Semantics annotation. On-Stack refactoring using released CDS views. Level B — only CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_OUTPUT (Classic API, Level B) identified as a finding. Replace with CDS annotation @Semantics.amount.currencyCode or direct ABAP string operation removing leading zeros. Replace REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY with Fiori Elements List Report. Custom Z-table ZFI_EBS_HEADER is customer-namespace and requires no modification. The report reads bank statement header data by date and GL account — this pattern aligns with standard I_BANKSTATEMENT CDS view, which should be evaluated as a potential base entity. Estimated effort: XS. The program is close to clean core compliance and is a low-risk on-stack remediation candidate.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_TRANFER_TICKETBank Accounting Document Transfer to Legacy General Ledger — A report that transfers SAP bank accounting document line items into an external legacy financial system as formatted debit/credit ticket transactions. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround BTP Side-by-Side via SAP Integration Suite. FMCA FM unreleased — must be replaced. Side-by-Side re-architecture using SAP Integration Suite — the only viable clean core path. Level D driven by EXEC SQL cross-database connection to external legacy GL system (LEGACY_SQLUSERTEST via DBCO), which is architecturally incompatible with the ABAP Cloud runtime. FMCA_CONVERT_PUT_SIGN_IN_FRONT (unreleased FM, Level C) must be replaced by a trivial mapping expression. Architecture: (1) expose ZFI_I_BANKS staging table as a released OData V4 service via ABAP Cloud RAP; (2) build SAP Integration Suite IFlow that subscribes to AccountingDocumentPosted business event, reads from ZFI_I_BANKS via OData, and inserts into legacy system via JDBC Adapter; (3) legacy system's GL_TKT_CONTROL ticket-sequence counter managed through JDBC adapter channel — eliminating direct EXEC SQL; (4) error handling via Integration Suite Alert Management replaces MESSAGE e888. Use SAP Connectivity Service + Cloud Connector as the network bridge for on-premise legacy database access. Estimated effort: L (33 days).
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_TRANSFER_LETTER_DEDUCBank Transfer Letter for Payroll and Social Insurance Deductions — A Finance report that generates an authorized, Adobe-form-based bank transfer letter for payroll and GOSI deduction payments, including Arabic amount-in-words rendering for Saudi Arabian payment processes. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed Hybrid. Replace TCURT with I_CURRENCYTEXT; Adobe Forms via SAP Forms Service on BTP. Hybrid clean core migration: on-stack refactoring plus side-by-side extension. Level D due to direct SELECT FROM TCURT (→ replace with I_CURRENCYTEXT CDS view for currency text, fully released). Architecture: (1) replace TCURT access with I_CURRENCYTEXT; (2) replace FP_JOB_OPEN/FP_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME/FP_JOB_CLOSE Adobe Forms pipeline with SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP for bilingual Arabic/English PDF generation; (3) replace SPELL_AMOUNT with a custom CAP/BTP amount-in-words utility supporting Arabic and English; (4) expose the signatory register ZFI_SIGNATURE as a managed custom entity; (5) use I_HR_FISCALDOCUMENT and Personnel Settlement Document Events for payroll deduction data instead of polling. Released CDS views identified: I_BANKPAYMENTDOCUMENT, I_HR_FISCALDOCUMENT, I_OUTGOINGCHECK, I_CURRENCYTEXT. The signatory registry and amount-in-words Arabic conversion require custom extension logic as no standard equivalent exists.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_TRANSFER_LETTER_DRAFTBank Transfer Letter Draft Generation for Saudi Payment Authorization — A Finance report that generates a formal Arabic-language bank transfer authorization letter as an Adobe Form PDF, populated with payment document data and authorized signatory details for Saudi Arabian treasury processes. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround Hybrid. Adobe FMs (FP_JOB_OPEN/CLOSE) are Level B — must move to BTP Forms Service. Side-by-Side BTP extension or hybrid On-Stack + BTP approach. Level D due to direct access to unreleased tables (TCURT → I_CURRENCYTEXT, and potentially ADRC/LFA1 → I_Supplier/I_BusinessPartnerAddress). All Adobe Forms FMs (FP_JOB_OPEN, FP_JOB_CLOSE, FP_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME) are Level B and must be replaced with SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP. Architecture: (1) data layer via released CDS views I_CURRENCYTEXT, I_Supplier, I_BusinessPartnerAddress; (2) signatory logic from custom ZFI_SIGNATURE table exposed as RAP entity; (3) bilingual Arabic/English PDF via SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP with custom XDP template; (4) SPELL_AMOUNT replaced by a BTP-side amount-in-words microservice. The output management framework should be used for document dispatch. Dual-signatory threshold logic and Arabic-script rendering require custom BTP extension — no standard Fiori app covers this specific bank transfer letter format.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_TRANSFER_LTR_DED_ELEBank Transfer Authorization Letter for Electricity Utility Payments — A Saudi Arabia-specific report that generates a bilingual Arabic/English bank transfer authorization letter instructing the company's bank to deduct a payment from the company account for electricity utility obligations. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround BTP Side-by-Side. Consistent pattern across SIDF bank transfer letter suite. Side-by-Side BTP extension for full clean core compliance. Level D due to unreleased table accesses and classic Adobe Forms API dependency. Pattern is consistent across the SIDF bank transfer letter suite. Architecture: (1) parked/posted document data via I_JOURNALENTRY OData API replacing VBKPF; (2) vendor/bank details via I_Supplier replacing LFA1; (3) currency text via I_CURRENCYTEXT replacing TCURT; (4) signatory registry via ZFI_SIGNATURE RAP entity; (5) bilingual Arabic/English electricity bill payment authorization letter PDF via SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP with custom XDP template; (6) SPELL_AMOUNT → BTP CAP microservice; (7) SPELL_AMOUNT Arabic Indic numerals rendered via BTP utility. Leverage Output Management framework for PDF dispatch. The amount-threshold-based multi-tier signature control (enforcing different signatories based on payment value) must be reimplemented in the BTP extension — no standard equivalent exists in S/4HANA output management.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_TRANSFER_LTR_DED_TELBank Transfer Letter for Telephone Bill Payment Authorization — A bilingual (English and Arabic) bank transfer authorization letter generator for telephone bill payments, with multi-tier signature controls based on payment amount thresholds. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed BTP Side-by-Side. Architecture: I_JOURNALENTRY + I_CURRENCYTEXT + SAP Forms on BTP. Side-by-Side BTP extension for clean core compliance. Level D due to unreleased table accesses (VBKPF, TCURT → I_CURRENCYTEXT) and FP_JOB_OPEN/CLOSE Adobe Forms pipeline (Level B). Architecture: (1) parked document data via I_JOURNALENTRY OData V4 replacing VBKPF; (2) currency text via I_CURRENCYTEXT CDS view; (3) bilingual English/Arabic bank transfer authorization letter PDF via SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP; (4) multi-tier signature control based on payment amount threshold reimplemented in BTP extension; (5) ZFI_SIGNATURE signatory table exposed as OData entity; (6) SPELL_AMOUNT → custom BTP Arabic amount-in-words function. The telephone bill payment authorization letter with Arabic-Indic numerals and dual-language rendering has no S/4HANA standard equivalent. SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP supports RTL Arabic layout in XDP templates. Estimated effort: L (refactoring of the entire letter-generation suite is recommended as a unified BTP project).
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_TRANSFER_LTR_EXT_ENExternal Bank Transfer Instruction Letter Generator — A bilingual (English/Arabic) SAP report that generates formal external bank transfer instruction letters in Adobe PDF format, used by the finance department to authorize outgoing payments to external beneficiaries. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed Hybrid. Replace all table reads with released CDS views; Adobe Forms to BTP Forms Service. Side-by-Side BTP extension for full clean core compliance. Level D due to unreleased table accesses and Adobe Forms classic API dependency. Architecture: (1) replace all direct table reads (VBKPF, LFA1, ADRC, TCURT) with released CDS views — I_JOURNALENTRY, I_Supplier, I_BusinessPartnerAddress, I_CURRENCYTEXT; (2) migrate from classic FP_JOB_OPEN/FP_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME/FP_JOB_CLOSE to SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP with bilingual XDP template; (3) SPELL_AMOUNT → custom BTP amount-in-words function supporting English and Arabic; (4) ZFI_SIGNATURE custom table exposed as RAP OData entity for signatory selection; (5) Output Management framework handles PDF dispatch to printer/email channel. The specific 'External Bank Transfer Instruction Letter' format — assembling accounting document data, vendor details, bank details, and authorized signatories into a legally formatted print-ready PDF — has no standard S/4HANA Fiori app equivalent and requires a custom BTP extension.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_TRANSFER_LTR_OF_CRDTBank Transfer Letter of Credit Generation — A Finance report that generates an official Adobe-based Bank Transfer Letter for Letter of Credit transactions, including accounting document data, customer and bank details, and authorized signatory information. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed Hybrid. LC data via I_BankGuarantee; vendor via I_Supplier; SAP Forms on BTP. Side-by-Side BTP extension or on-stack ABAP Cloud RAP app. Level D due to unreleased table accesses (AKKP, AKKB Letter of Credit tables → I_BankGuarantee CDS successor; LFA1 → I_Supplier; TCURT → I_CURRENCYTEXT) and Adobe Forms classic API (FP_JOB_OPEN/CLOSE — Level B). Architecture: (1) LC/BG data via I_BankGuarantee CDS view replacing AKKP/AKKB; (2) vendor name and bank details via I_Supplier and I_BusinessPartnerBankDetails replacing LFA1; (3) accounting document data via I_JOURNALENTRY replacing direct FI document reads; (4) currency text via I_CURRENCYTEXT replacing TCURT; (5) dual-signatory control via ZFI_SIGNATURE custom entity; (6) bilingual Arabic/English PDF via SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP; (7) SPELL_AMOUNT → BTP amount-in-words utility. The custom signatory register, amount-in-words Arabic conversion, and LC-specific letter format require custom extension — no standard output management template covers this scenario.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BANK_TRANSFR_LETTR_EMP_ALWBank Transfer Letter Generation for Employee Allowance Payments — A program that generates official, signed bank transfer instruction letters for employee allowance and GOSI payments, combining accounting documents, HR master data, and bank details into a formatted Adobe Forms PDF output. —Fully Available as part of the Standard BTP Side-by-Side. Full rebuild using released APIs + SAP Forms Service. All Level D eliminated. Full side-by-Side BTP extension using released APIs, Fiori UI, and SAP Forms Service by Adobe. Level D due to direct access to unreleased tables: VBKPF, VBSEGS, SKB1 (accounting batch input/parked documents), PA0001, PA0009 (HR infotypes — not available on S/4HANA Public Cloud as PA tables don't exist in cloud HCM), T012K, TCURT, ADRC. Architecture: (1) accounting data via released OData API for accounting documents (VBKPF/VBSEGS → Parked Journal Entry APIs); (2) employee data retrieved from SuccessFactors via SAP Integration Suite (PA0001/PA0009 → SuccessFactors Employee Central OData APIs); (3) PDF bank transfer letter via SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP; (4) ZFI_COMP_TEXT and ZFI_SIGNATURE custom tables migrated to BTP as custom database tables or CDS entities in BTP ABAP environment; (5) SPELL_AMOUNT → custom BTP utility; (6) dual-authorization control for amounts exceeding SAR 50,000 implemented via workflow in SAP Build Process Automation. All Level D and Level B findings eliminated. The HR PA infotype dependency is a critical blocker for on-stack approaches.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BCM_ATTACHMENT_DELETEBCM Payment Batch Attachment Deletion Utility — A report allowing authorized treasury and finance administrators to review and selectively delete workflow-linked file attachments from SAP Bank Communication Management payment batches. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround Hybrid. No released OData API for BCM batch attachment deletion — phased hybrid migration. Phased hybrid migration (S+O). Level D driven by a single disqualifier: direct DELETE FROM SWW_CONTOB (SAP-owned standard table). There is no released OData V2/V4 API on SAP API Business Hub for workflow attachment deletion for BCM batches. Track 1 — On-Stack interim refactoring for S/4HANA Private Cloud: (1) Replace REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY with RAP-based Fiori Elements List Report; (2) replace SWWWIHEAD direct SELECT with I_WORKFLOWSTATUSOVERVIEW CDS view; (3) replace CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT/OUTPUT with ABAP Cloud-compliant logic; (4) document DELETE FROM SWW_CONTOB as a controlled clean core exception with dedicated Z_BCM_ATTACH_DEL authorization object; (5) fix the commented-out wi_id filter bug to prevent over-deletion. Track 2 — Side-by-Side BTP (future state): implement CAP or ABAP Cloud RAP app once SAP delivers a released workflow attachment management API. Monitor SAP API Business Hub for new BCM attachment APIs with each quarterly release. Run ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) with Cloud Readiness check variant after refactoring.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BCM_STATUS_UPDATEBCM Payment Batch Approval Status Override — A program enabling treasury and finance administrators to manually force the approval status of Bank Communication Management payment batches and their line items to either Accepted or Rejected, bypassing the standard approval workflow. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround BTP Side-by-Side. Replace BNK_BATCH_HEADER reads with released payment batch OData API. Side-by-Side BTP extension as primary path for S/4HANA Public Cloud. Level D due to direct SELECT/MODIFY on BNK_BATCH_HEADER and CRM_JEST tables, and VRM_SET_VALUES (Classic API). Architecture: (1) replace BNK_BATCH_HEADER reads with released payment batch OData API; (2) replace CRM_JEST MODIFY with Object Status Management API (OData V2) or STATUS_CHANGE_EXTERN function module (released via STAS function group) mapping IBC17 (Accepted) and IBC19 (Rejected); (3) replace classic selection screen with RAP-based Fiori Elements app with OData V4 binding; (4) use SAP Integration Suite iFlow to trigger the status update from external systems. On-Stack refactoring is conditionally viable for Private Cloud if Object Status Management API is confirmed as the write mechanism. Once BTP extension is in production, decommission ZFI_BCM_STATUS_UPDATE immediately — concurrent use creates inconsistent states.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BID_SUMMARIZE_REPORTTreasury Bid and Offer Summarize Report with PDF Print — A SAP Treasury Management report that displays a consolidated view of active financial market bids and offers, showing counterparty details, deal terms, and approval status, with the ability to print selected bids as a PDF document. —Fully Available as part of the Standard BTP Side-by-Side. On-Stack refactor using released ABAP Cloud APIs + Fiori Elements. Read-only. On-Stack refactoring using released ABAP Cloud APIs and Fiori Elements, or evaluate retirement against standard SAP analytics. Level C due to direct SELECT on SAP Treasury/Risk Management tables (VTBCO, VTBFINP, VTBFHA, BUT000) and REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY (Level B). Replace: (1) VTBCO/VTBFINP/VTBFHA → I_TreasuryTransaction CDS views (evaluate I_BankContractTransaction or I_FinancialTransaction depending on product type); (2) BUT000 Business Partner name resolution → I_BusinessPartner CDS view; (3) REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY → Fiori Elements List Report via RAP OData V4 service. The report is read-only with no write operations — low-risk refactoring candidate. SAP Analytics Cloud can serve as the target analytics layer for bid summarization, consuming released TRM CDS views. Consider retirement evaluation against standard SAP Treasury Fiori apps before investing in custom migration.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BTL_EXT_BANK_LETTERExternal Bank Transfer Letter Generation with Authorized Signatory Selection — A program to generate formal external bank transfer authorization letters by linking parked FI accounting documents to two dynamically selected, date-validated authorized signatories. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround Hybrid. On-stack refactor for data extraction; SAP Forms Service on BTP for form generation. On-stack refactor of data extraction to released APIs; form generation to be migrated to SAP Forms Service by Adobe via BTP.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BUDGET_CHANGEBudget Transfer Record Maintenance and Correction Utility — An administrative tool to delete or reassign fund centers on custom budget transfer records in SAP Funds Management. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround BTP ABAP Cloud Side-by-Side. Only gap is the classic ABAP report architecture itself. Side-by-Side BTP ABAP Cloud extension. Level B — the program is remarkably clean, touching only customer-owned Z-tables (ZFI_BUDGET_T, ZFI_BUDGET_T_I, ZFI_BUDGET_T_ID) and calling no SAP standard APIs. The only gap is the classic ABAP report architecture itself. Target architecture: implement a RAP Business Object representing the budget transfer entity backed by the same Z-tables, expose Delete and Update actions as OData V4 operations, and surface through a Fiori Elements UI with proper authorization checks. This delivers full ABAP Cloud compliance (Level A), a governed and auditable correction workflow, and upgrade stability. Add explicit change logging via application log (CL_BAL_LOG) to ensure traceable administrative corrections — the current program has no audit trail. Estimated effort: XS–S (the logic is simple; the main work is the RAP/Fiori scaffolding).
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BUDGET_PERIOD_REPORTBudget Period Report for SAP Funds Management — A multi-screen interactive report displaying budget consumption status across consolidated, consumable budget, committed expenditure, and actual expenditure perspectives, broken down by fund center and commitment item across fiscal periods. —Fully Available as part of the Standard Hybrid. On-Stack refactoring (primary). No Level D violations. On-Stack refactoring (primary) or Side-by-Side BTP extension. Level C due to direct access to unreleased FM internal tables inside ZFI_BUDGET_PERIOD_REPORT function module: FMIFIIT and FMIOI (neither found in the SAP Cloudification Repository as released objects). No Level D violations exist. Replace: (1) direct FM table reads with released CDS views — I_PUBSECFNDSMBDGTACTLCUBE, I_PUBSECBDGTACTLITEMCUBE, C_PUBSECBDGTACTLITEMQRY1 (Public Edition; avoid PCE_I_ prefix views for Public Cloud); (2) REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY + 4 dynpro screens → Fiori Elements Analytical List Page with tab navigation; (3) double-click drill-down → Fiori Elements object page navigation. Before migration, confirm whether PSM-FM is available in the target public cloud tenant. No business events found for FM budget/actuals/commitments — real-time push architecture is not yet feasible; the reimplemented report must remain pull-based. Budget period master data available via data product sap-s4com-BudgetPeriod-v1.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BUDGET_POSTINGBudget Transfer Posting to SAP Funds Management — A program that reads approved budget transfer requests from custom staging tables and posts them as official FM budget transfer documents in SAP using BAPI_0050_CREATE, then updates the staging records with the resulting document numbers and posting status. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed Hybrid. Use released FM CDS views + OData V2 API for budget creation (replace BAPI_0050_CREATE). Hybrid S+O strategy. Level C driven by two violations: direct access to FMIFIIT and FMIOI inside ZFI_BUDGET_PERIOD_REPORT function module (Level C — unreleased SAP FM internal tables), and BAPI_0050_CREATE (Classic API, Level B). Released CDS views to replace direct table accesses: I_FMBudgetDocument, I_FMBudgetLineItem, I_FMOpenItem, C_FMBudgetTransferAnalytics. Released OData V2 API for FM budget document creation was identified — use to replace BAPI_0050_CREATE. On-Stack path (O): refactor ZFI_BUDGET_PERIOD_REPORT function module to consume released CDS views; replace BAPI_0050_CREATE with released FM document creation API. Side-by-Side path (S): re-architect the approval workflow and period-distribution algorithm as a SAP Build Process Automation flow on BTP, integrating with S/4HANA via released APIs. The custom period-level greedy fill algorithm and multi-table staging lifecycle (ZFI_BUDGET_H/T/T_I) have no standard equivalent — Retire (R) is not recommended.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BUDGET_REPORTSAP Funds Management Budget Tracking and Monitoring Report — An interactive Funds Management budget report that provides real-time visibility into consumable, actual, and committed budget figures by funds center and commitment item, with drill-down capability to individual transaction detail. —Fully Available as part of the Standard BTP Side-by-Side. On-Stack RAP backed by released FM CDS analytical views. No write ops. On-Stack refactoring to ABAP Cloud (RAP) backed by released FM CDS analytical views, surfaced as a Fiori Elements Analytical List Page. Level C — entirely driven by direct SELECT access to FM internal tables. No write operations exist. Released CDS views: I_PUBSECFNDSMBDGTACTLCUBE (Public Edition), I_PUBSECBDGTACTLITEMCUBE, C_PUBSECBDGTACTLITEMQRY1. On-Stack ABAP Cloud RAP implementation on BTP ABAP Environment is the recommended target — retaining ABAP skills while achieving full cloud compliance. The S/4HANA standard proximity score of 4 indicates strong standard coverage. Before committing to custom migration, complete a brief retirement assessment against standard SAP FM Fiori apps (proximity score 4 not 5, leaving room for a partial gap). Replace REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY and all ALV screens with Fiori Elements Analytical List Page. The print action (SUBMIT RFFMED_PRINT) maps to Fiori's Output Management integration.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_BUDGET_REPORT2Budget Status Report for Funds Management (Multi-Screen Drilldown) — An interactive SAP Funds Management budget status report providing a hierarchical breakdown of consumable, actual, committed, and accumulated-committed budget figures by fund center and commitment item across four drilldown ALV screens. Released CDS Views (FM Analytical)released CDS views mapping directly to all four problematic data sourcesFully Available as part of the Standard On-Stack ABAP Cloud. Proximity 4/5. Direct mapping to released FM CDS views. On-Stack refactoring to ABAP Cloud RAP backed by released FM CDS analytical views. Level C — read-only report with no writes against SAP standard tables, making remediation scope tight and low-risk. Proximity score 4/5 with strong standard coverage and public-edition released CDS views mapping directly to all four problematic data sources. Replace: (1) four direct FM table SELECTs → I_PUBSECFNDSMBDGTACTLCUBE, I_PUBSECBDGTACTLITEMCUBE, C_PUBSECBDGTACTLITEMQRY1, C_FMBudgetTransferAnalytics; (2) four ALV screens (budget summary, consumed, committed, accumulated) → Fiori Elements tab pages or navigation targets in a single Fiori app; (3) drill-down double-click → Fiori Elements object-page navigation; (4) print action → Fiori Output Management integration removing SUBMIT RFFMED_PRINT dependency. Business logic (aggregation, text enrichment, hierarchical rollup) fully preserved in RAP projection layer or ABAP Cloud calculation logic class. AI enhancement: Analytical Business Insights (generative AI) for natural language querying and anomaly commentary can be layered on top.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_CASH_MANAGEMENT_SUMMARYWeekly Cash Management Summary Report — A treasury report that consolidates weekly cash positions including account balances, investment maturities, disbursements, and operating expenses for management review and PDF output. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed Hybrid. Full re-architecture via RAP APIs + BTP Side-by-Side. Treasury data via I_FinancialTransaction. Full re-architecture using RAP-based APIs and a Side-by-Side BTP extension. Level D due to direct reads on VTBFHAZU and VTBFHAPO (SAP Treasury/Risk Management internal tables — no CDS successors confirmed for these specific payment flow tables), FP_JOB_OPEN/CLOSE (Level B Adobe Forms), and BUT000 business partner table (→ I_BusinessPartner). Architecture: (1) cash balance data from custom Z-tables ZFI_CASH_HEADER and ZFI_CASH_BALANCE via RAP OData V4 (customer namespace — already clean); (2) treasury investment data (fixed deposits, Murabaha instruments) via I_FinancialTransaction or I_CashLiquidityItem CDS views replacing VTBFHAZU/VTBFHAPO direct reads; (3) business partner name → I_BusinessPartner replacing BUT000; (4) weekly PDF cash management report → SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP; (5) dual-calendar (Gregorian + Hijri Islamic) output requires a BTP-side Hijri conversion utility. Estimated effort: XL (complex multi-domain consolidation report with Islamic finance instrument specifics).
Finance (Custom) ZFI_CHEQUE_AND_CASH_RECEIPTCheque and Cash Receipt Printing for Incoming Payments — A bilingual (English and Arabic) SAP FI report that retrieves incoming payment records by receipt number and generates a printed Adobe Forms receipt — either a cash receipt or a cheque receipt — with two copies: one for the customer and one for the organisation. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround BTP Side-by-Side. CAP or ABAP Cloud + I_JOURNALENTRY + bilingual PDF via BTP Adobe Document Services. Side-by-Side BTP extension using CAP or ABAP Cloud, consuming only released APIs, with bilingual PDF via SAP BTP Adobe Document Services. Level D due to direct SELECT on BKPF (→ I_JOURNALENTRY or I_JOURNALENTRYLINEITEM) and KNA1 (→ I_CUSTOMER — Not To Be Released). Short-term: replace BKPF with I_JOURNALENTRY and KNA1 with I_CUSTOMER to lift code to Level B; however, the correct strategic approach for Public Cloud is a full BTP CAP or ABAP Cloud side-by-side extension. Architecture: (1) expose ZFI_I_INCOM_PAY as RAP OData V4 service; (2) build CAP service aggregating data from ZFI_I_INCOM_PAY, I_JOURNALENTRY, I_CUSTOMER; (3) implement amount-to-words in English and Arabic as a CAP custom utility (SPELL_AMOUNT has no ABAP Cloud successor); (4) redesign ZFI_CASH_FORM and ZFI_CHEQUE_FORM as bilingual templates in Adobe Document Services on BTP; (5) output via API_CLOUD_PRINT_PULL_SRV. No standard S/4HANA app provides bilingual dual-copy cash/cheque receipt printing (proximity 2/10). Estimated effort: L.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_COST_DISTRIBUTIONSalary Cost Distribution Across Departmental Cost Centers — An SAP FI/HR integration report that automatically calculates and distributes salary-related costs across departmental cost centers based on active employee headcount, then parks the resulting accounting document in SAP Financial Accounting for SIDF. Manage Allocation CyclesAvailable — Standard & BTP Extension Needed BTP Side-by-Side. On-Stack refactor using released ABAP Cloud APIs. On-Stack refactoring using released ABAP Cloud APIs. Evaluate the specific table and FM violations to determine exact effort. Replace all direct SELECT statements on SAP standard tables with released CDS views in the CO/FI domain. Replace REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY with Fiori Elements List Report. If the program posts FI/CO documents, replace BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST with I_JOURNALENTRYTP behavior definition via EML. If the program reads cost center master data, use I_COSTCENTER CDS view. The cost distribution logic (allocation rules, distribution percentages) should be preserved in a RAP Business Object action. Evaluate standard CO allocation cycles (SAP Fiori apps 'Manage Allocation Cycles') as a potential retire-and-replace path for the core distribution logic.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_DEF_DOC_UPDATEDeferred Income Schedule Upload for Loan Disbursements — A finance upload program that reads loan disbursement data from Excel and generates monthly deferred income recognition entries in SAP for Pre-EMI Charges. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround BTP Side-by-Side. Replace Excel upload (Level B) with Fiori file upload; Hijri conversion on BTP. Side-by-Side BTP extension (primary for Public Cloud) or On-Stack ABAP Cloud RAP (for Private Cloud). Level B with re-architecture required to eliminate SAPGUI and classic API dependencies. Architecture: (1) replace Excel upload (TEXT_CONVERT_XLS_TO_SAP — Level B) with Fiori Elements file upload facet or BTP CAP CSV/XLSX processing; (2) the Hijri-to-Gregorian date conversion (cl_abap_datfm=>conv_isl_to_greg_formula) should be validated in ABAP Cloud — if not available, implement a BTP-side Hijri utility; (3) CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT → CDS annotation or ABAP Cloud-compliant leading-zero padding; (4) MODIFY zfi_def_inc FROM TABLE → preserve as customer-namespace operation; (5) ensure single-commit transactional boundary (current program has a two-phase commit risk between BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT and COMMIT WORK). Consider leveraging the standard Deferred Income OData V2 API and I_DeferredIncomeSchedule CDS view for the posting lifecycle downstream.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_DEF_DOC_UPDATE2Deferred Income Data Upload with Hijri-to-Gregorian Date Conversion — A program to load deferred income installment data from an Excel spreadsheet into SAP, converting Islamic calendar dates to Gregorian and calculating round-off adjustments for loan portfolios. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround Hybrid. On-Stack refactor for ABAP Cloud compliance; retire after single execution. On-Stack refactoring for ABAP Cloud compliance, then retire after single execution. Level B — the program touches only the customer-owned Z-table ZFI_DEF_INC and performs a straightforward MODIFY FROM TABLE upsert. Remediation steps: (1) add explicit ROLLBACK WORK handling for sy-subrc ≠ 0 (current program silently abandons on failure — critical gap); (2) implement application logging via CL_BAL_LOG to record timestamp, user, counts, and outcome for finance audit requirements; (3) add test-run mode (p_test parameter) to preview records before productive execution; (4) use SAVEPOINT/ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT pattern for atomicity; (5) replace CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT with ABAP Cloud-compliant equivalent. Execute in QAS first with copy of production data, then transport to production. After successful execution and verification, retire the program — it is a one-time data correction utility with no ongoing value.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_DEF_INC_TAB_UPDZFI_DEF_INC_TAB_UPD — Deferred Income Table Data Correction – Company Code Fix
A one-time data correction program that identifies deferred income entries with a blank company code in the ZFI_DEF_INC table and ...		 —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround On-Stack ABAP Cloud. Add SAVEPOINT/ROLLBACK + CL_BAL_LOG; retire after one-time correction. On-Stack refactoring for ABAP Cloud compliance. Level B — program operates exclusively on customer-owned Z-table ZFI_DEF_INC and performs a delete/re-insert correction for blank BUKRS records. Remediation: (1) use SAVEPOINT before the DELETE phase, execute DELETE, execute INSERT in the same LUW, issue single COMMIT WORK only after both succeed, issue ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT on any failure; (2) add application logging via CL_BAL_LOG recording execution timestamp, user, count of affected records, and outcome; (3) add test-run parameter for QAS validation before production execution. Standard interfaces available: Deferred Income OData V2 API, I_DeferredIncomeSchedule CDS view, Deferred Income Schedule Line Changed business event, Deferred Income Data Correction Integration Suite package. No direct SAP Business AI service matches this one-time data correction requirement. After successful production execution and verification, retire this utility.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_DEFERRED_INCOME_POSTDeferred Income Monthly Recognition and Parking Post — A program that automates the monthly recognition of deferred income for loan or financing products by creating and parking General Ledger accounting documents for a selected company, fiscal year, and accounting period. Journal Entry Posting Approval with AutomationsFully Available as part of the Standard Hybrid. Replace BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST with I_JOURNALENTRYTP via EML; externalize hardcoded G/L accounts. On-Stack RAP ABAP Cloud refactoring with Side-by-Side elements for workflow orchestration. Level D due to BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST (→ I_JOURNALENTRYTP via EML) and direct ZFI_DEF_INC table updates with COMMIT WORK violations. Architecture: (1) replace BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST with EML call to I_JOURNALENTRYTP for journal entry parking; (2) externalize hardcoded GL accounts (0022001010, 0031002010), cost center (CR1000), currency (SAR), and fiscal year variant (SA) into a configuration table keyed by company code; (3) replace two-phase commit (BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT in Phase 5 + COMMIT WORK in Phase 6) with single RAP SAVE_SEQUENCE or COMMIT ENTITIES boundary to eliminate duplicate posting risk; (4) fix the commented-out per-record loop so each ZFI_DEF_INC record receives its own document number; (5) leverage SAP Build Process Automation template 'Journal Entry Posting Approval with Automations' for period-end orchestration. AI augmentation: SAP Accounting Accruals Agent for anomaly detection on periodic recognition amounts and pre-population of deferral proposals.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_DEL_DUPL_BUDGET_RESERVDuplicate Budget Reservation Deletion for Employee Travel Requests — A Finance utility report to identify and remove duplicate or erroneous Funds Management budget reservations linked to employee business and training trips by zeroing out their reserved amounts via the standard SAP Funds Management API. —Not Available as SaaS — Full BTP Extension as a Workaround Hybrid. Refactor on-stack using released FM APIs. Add duplicate detection + approval control. Refactor on-stack using released FM APIs. Add proper duplicate detection logic and approval control. Side-by-side for enhanced travel request integration.
Finance (Custom) ZFI_DMS_DOCUMENT_LISTAccounting Document DMS Attachment List Report — A report that displays accounting documents alongside their Document Management System attachments, allowing users to identify which financial documents have associated electronic files and which are missing supporting documentation. —Fully Available as part of the Standard BTP Side-by-Side. I_JOURNALENTRY + API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV replacing BKPF. Side-by-Side BTP extension (primary for Public Cloud) or On-Stack ABAP Cloud RAP (secondary for Private Cloud/On-Premise). Level D due to BKPF (Not To Be Released → I_JOURNALENTRY or API_JOURNALENTRY_SRV OData V4) and REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY (Dynpro ALV — no ABAP Cloud equivalent). Architecture: (1) accounting document data via I_JOURNALENTRY CDS view / API_JOURNALENTRY_SRV OData V4 replacing BKPF SELECT; (2) DMS attachment links via API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV (verify BKPF-linked object scope) or I_DOCINFORECORDACCMSECIDASGT to traverse from business object key to Document Info Record, replacing DRAD direct access; (3) UI rebuilt as Fiori Elements List Report on BTP. Critical pre-implementation validation: (a) confirm API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV supports querying by object type BKPF with composite key (company code + document number + fiscal year); (b) confirm AccountingDocumentCategory mapping for bstat codes (Z, V, W) in I_JOURNALENTRY. No standard S/4HANA app provides this consolidated cross-domain view (accounting documents with/without DMS attachments — proximity 4/10).
Finance (Custom) ZFI_E_AND_Y_FORMSBilingual Statutory Financial Statements Generator for SIDF — A program that generates the four primary statutory financial statements (Balance Sheet, Statement of Financial Position, Statement of Changes in Equity, and Cash Flow) for SIDF in a bilingual Arabic/English Adobe PDF format, supporting Hijri calendar dates and IFRS-compliant presentation. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed BTP Side-by-Side. I_BalanceSheetAccountCube + I_FinancialStatementItem; Hijri via BTP utility. Complete Side-by-Side BTP redevelopment. Level D with multiple unreleased object dependencies and no standard S/4HANA equivalent for simultaneous bilingual Arabic/English IFRS statutory financial statement generation with Hijri calendar support. Architecture: (1) financial data via released CDS views — I_GLACCOUNT, I_BalanceSheetAccountCube, I_GLAccountLineItemRawData, I_FinancialStatementItem for Balance Sheet, P&L, Statement of Changes in Equity, and Cash Flow; (2) Hijri date conversion via BTP-side utility (mature open-source Hijri libraries available in Node.js/NPM ecosystem); (3) bilingual Arabic/English PDF with RTL Arabic layout via SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP using custom XDP templates; (4) IFRS-compliant presentation and Saudi CMA audit formatting in custom BTP extension. The dual-language simultaneous output, Hijri calendar dates, and SOCPA/IFRS-compliant statutory statement format are SIDF-specific requirements not covered by any S/4HANA standard Fiori app. Estimated effort: XL (complex regulatory output with dual-calendar and dual-language requirements).
Finance (Custom) ZFI_EMP_SAVINGS_PRGMEmployee Savings Contributions Form — A Finance module report that collects employee payroll posting data and GL account balances to generate a printable Adobe PDF form showing each employee's savings contributions per General Ledger account for a selected posting date range. —Available — Standard & BTP Extension Needed Hybrid. Replace SKAT with I_GLACCOUNTTEXT; investigate released payroll API alternatives.
